Name: APlayerCameraManager
Description:
A PlayerCameraManager is responsible for managing the camera for a particular
  player. It defines the final view properties used by other systems (e.g. the renderer),
  meaning you can think of it as your virtual eyeball in the world. It can compute the 
  final camera properties directly, or it can arbitrateblend between other objects or 
  actors that influence the camera (e.g. blending from one CameraActor to another).
  
  The PlayerCameraManagers primary external responsibility is to reliably respond to
  various Get() functions, such as GetCameraViewPoint. Most everything else is
  implementation detail and overrideable by user projects.
  
  By default, a PlayerCameraManager maintains a "view target", which is the primary actor
  the camera is associated with. It can also apply various "post" effects to the final 
  view state, such as camera animations, shakes, post-process effects or special 
  effects such as dirt on the lens.

Variables.Name: PCOwner
Variables.Description: PlayerController that owns this Camera actor

Variables.Name: TransformComponent
Variables.Description: Dummy component we can use to attach things to the camera.

Variables.Name: DefaultFOV
Variables.Description: FOV to use by default.

Variables.Name: DefaultOrthoWidth
Variables.Description: The default desired width (in world units) of the orthographic view (ignored in Perspective mode)

Variables.Name: DefaultAspectRatio
Variables.Description: Default aspect ratio (used when a view target override the aspect ratio and bConstrainAspectRatio is set; most of the time the value from a camera component will be used instead)

Variables.Name: CameraCache
Variables.Description: Cached camera properties.

Variables.Name: LastFrameCameraCache
Variables.Description: Cached camera properties, one frame old.

Variables.Name: ViewTarget
Variables.Description: Current ViewTarget

Variables.Name: PendingViewTarget
Variables.Description: Pending view target for blending

Variables.Name: CachedViewPOV
Variables.Description: If This POV is not null, Use this Value to Blend Target

Variables.Name: ModifierList
Variables.Description: List of active camera modifier instances that have a chance to update the final camera POV

Variables.Name: DefaultModifiers
Variables.Description: List of modifiers to create by default for this camera

Variables.Name: FreeCamDistance
Variables.Description: Distance to place free camera from view target (used in certain CameraStyles)

Variables.Name: FreeCamOffset
Variables.Description: Offset to Z free camera position (used in certain CameraStyles)

Variables.Name: ViewTargetOffset
Variables.Description: Offset to view target (used in certain CameraStyles)

Variables.Name: CameraLensEffects
Variables.Description: CameraBlood emitter attached to this camera

Variables.Name: CachedCameraShakeMod
Variables.Description: Cached ref to modifier for code-driven screen shakes

Variables.Name: AnimInstPool
Variables.Description: Internal pool of camera anim instance objects available for playing camera animations. Defines the max number of camera anims that can play simultaneously.

Variables.Name: PostProcessBlendCache
Variables.Description:
Internal pool of camera anim instance objects available for playing camera animations. Defines the max number of camera anims that can play simultaneously. 
	class UCameraAnimInst AnimInstPool[8];    MAX_ACTIVE_CAMERA_ANIMS @fixme constant 
	 Internal list of active post process effects. Parallel array to PostProcessBlendCacheWeights.

Variables.Name: ActiveAnims
Variables.Description: Array of camera anim instances that are currently playing and in-use

Variables.Name: FreeAnims
Variables.Description: Array of camera anim instances that are not playing and available to be used.

Variables.Name: AnimCameraActor
Variables.Description: Internal. Receives the output of individual camera animations.

Variables.Name: bIsOrthographic
Variables.Description: True when this camera should use an orthographic perspective instead of FOV

Variables.Name: bDefaultConstrainAspectRatio
Variables.Description: True if black bars should be added if the destination view has a different aspect ratio (only used when a view target doesn't specify whether or not to constrain the aspect ratio; most of the time the value from a camera component is used instead)

Variables.Name: bUseClientSideCameraUpdates
Variables.Description: True if server will use camera positions replicated from the client instead of calculating them locally.

Variables.Name: bGameCameraCutThisFrame
Variables.Description:
True if we did a camera cut this frame. Automatically reset to false every frame.
	  This flag affects various things in the renderer (such as whether to use the occlusion queries from last frame, and motion blur).

Variables.Name: ViewPitchMin
Variables.Description: Minimum view pitch, in degrees.

Variables.Name: ViewPitchMax
Variables.Description: Maximum view pitch, in degrees.

Variables.Name: ViewYawMin
Variables.Description: Minimum view yaw, in degrees.

Variables.Name: ViewYawMax
Variables.Description: Maximum view yaw, in degrees.

Variables.Name: ViewRollMin
Variables.Description: Minimum view roll, in degrees.

Variables.Name: ViewRollMax
Variables.Description: Maximum view roll, in degrees.

Functions.Name: PhotographyCameraModify
Functions.Description:
Implementable blueprint hook to allow a PlayerCameraManager subclass to
	 constrain or otherwise modify the camera during free-camera photography.
	 For example, a blueprint may wish to limit the distance from the camera's
	 original point, or forbid the camera from passing through walls.
	 NewCameraLocation contains the proposed new camera location.
	 PreviousCameraLocation contains the camera location in the previous frame.
	 OriginalCameraLocation contains the camera location before the game was put
	 into photography mode.
	 Return ResultCameraLocation as modified according to your constraints.

Functions.Name: OnPhotographySessionStart
Functions.Description:
Event triggered upon entering Photography mode (before pausing, if
	 r.Photography.AutoPause is 1).

Functions.Name: OnPhotographySessionEnd
Functions.Description:
Event triggered upon leaving Photography mode (after unpausing, if
	 r.Photography.AutoPause is 1).

Functions.Name: OnPhotographyMultiPartCaptureStart
Functions.Description:
Event triggered upon the start of a multi-part photograph capture (i.e. a
	 stereoscopic or 360-degree shot).  This is an ideal time to turn off
	 rendering effects that tile badly (UI, subtitles, vignette, very aggressive
	 bloom, etc; most of these are automatically disabled when
	 r.Photography.AutoPostprocess is 1).

Functions.Name: OnPhotographyMultiPartCaptureEnd
Functions.Description:
Event triggered upon the end of a multi-part photograph capture, when manual
	 free-roaming photographic camera control is about to be returned to the user.
	 Here you may re-enable whatever was turned off within
	 OnPhotographyMultiPartCaptureStart.

Functions.Name: BlueprintUpdateCamera
Functions.Description:
Blueprint hook to allow blueprints to override existing camera behavior or implement custom cameras.
	  If this function returns true, we will use the given returned values and skip further calculations to determine
	  final camera POV.

Functions.Name: GetOwningPlayerController
Functions.Description: Returns the PlayerController that owns this camera.

Functions.Name: AddNewCameraModifier
Functions.Description: Creates and initializes a new camera modifier of the specified class.

Params.Name: ModifierClass
Params.Description: - The class of camera modifier to create.

Return.Description: Returns the newly created camera modifier.

Functions.Name: FindCameraModifierByClass
Functions.Description:
Returns camera modifier for this camera of the given class, if it exists. 
	  Exact class match only. If there are multiple modifiers of the same class, the first one is returned.

Functions.Name: RemoveCameraModifier
Functions.Description: Removes the given camera modifier from this camera (if it's on the camera in the first place) and discards it.

Return.Description: True if successfully removed, false otherwise.
Returns the camera's current full FOV angle, in degrees.

Functions.Name: SetFOV
Functions.Description: Locks the FOV to the given value.  Unlock with UnlockFOV.

Params.Name: NewFOV
Params.Description: - New full FOV angle to use, in degrees.

Functions.Name: UnlockFOV
Functions.Description: Unlocks the FOV.

Functions.Name: AddCameraLensEffect
Functions.Description: Creates a camera lens effect of the given class on this camera.

Params.Name: LensEffectEmitterClass
Params.Description: - Class of lens effect emitter to create.

Functions.Name: RemoveCameraLensEffect
Functions.Description: Removes the given lens effect from the camera.

Params.Name: Emitter
Params.Description: - the emitter actor to remove from the camera

Functions.Name: ClearCameraLensEffects
Functions.Description: Removes all camera lens effects.

Functions.Name: PlayCameraShake
Functions.Description: Plays a camera shake on this camera.

Params.Name: Scale
Params.Description: - Scalar defining how "intense" to play the shake. 1.0 is normal (as authored).

Params.Name: PlaySpace
Params.Description: - Which coordinate system to play the shake in (affects oscillations and camera anims)

Params.Name: UserPlaySpaceRot
Params.Description: - Coordinate system to play shake when PlaySpace == CAPS_UserDefined.

Functions.Name: StopCameraShake
Functions.Description: Immediately stops the given shake instance and invalidates it.

Functions.Name: StopAllInstancesOfCameraShake
Functions.Description: Stops playing CameraShake of the given class.

Functions.Name: StopAllCameraShakes
Functions.Description: Stops all active camera shakes on this camera.

Functions.Name: StartCameraFade
Functions.Description: Does a camera fade tofrom a solid color.  Animates automatically.

Params.Name: FromAlpha
Params.Description: - Alpha at which to begin the fade. Range [0..1], where 0 is fully transparent and 1 is fully opaque solid color.

Params.Name: ToAlpha
Params.Description: - Alpha at which to finish the fade.

Params.Name: Duration
Params.Description: - How long the fade should take, in seconds.

Params.Name: Color
Params.Description: - Color to fade tofrom.

Params.Name: bShouldFadeAudio
Params.Description: - True to fade audio volume along with the alpha of the solid color.

Params.Name: bHoldWhenFinished
Params.Description: - True for fade to hold at the ToAlpha until explicitly stopped (e.g. with StopCameraFade)

Functions.Name: StopCameraFade
Functions.Description: Stops camera fading.

Functions.Name: SetManualCameraFade
Functions.Description:
Turns on camera fading at the given opacity. Does not auto-animate, allowing user to animate themselves.
	  Call StopCameraFade to turn fading back off.

Functions.Name: PlayCameraAnim
Functions.Description: Play the indicated CameraAnim on this camera.

Params.Name: Anim
Params.Description: The animation that should play on this instance.

Params.Name: Rate
Params.Description: How fast to play the animation. 1.0 is normal.

Params.Name: Scale
Params.Description: How "intense" to play the animation. 1.0 is normal.

Params.Name: BlendInTime
Params.Description: Time to linearly ramp in.

Params.Name: BlendOutTime
Params.Description: Time to linearly ramp out.

Params.Name: bLoop
Params.Description: True to loop the animation if it hits the end.

Params.Name: bRandomStartTime
Params.Description: Whether or not to choose a random time to start playing. Useful with bLoop=true and a duration to randomize things like shakes.

Params.Name: Duration
Params.Description: Optional total playtime for this animation, including blends. 0 means to use animations natural duration, or infinite if looping.

Params.Name: PlaySpace
Params.Description: Which space to play the animation in.

Params.Name: UserPlaySpaceRot
Params.Description: Custom play space, used when PlaySpace is UserDefined.

Return.Description: The CameraAnim instance, which can be stored to manipulatestop the anim after the fact.

Functions.Name: StopAllInstancesOfCameraAnim
Functions.Description: Stop playing all instances of the indicated CameraAnim.

Params.Name: bImmediate
Params.Description: True to stop it right now and ignore blend out, false to let it blend out as indicated.

Functions.Name: StopCameraAnimInst
Functions.Description: Stops the given CameraAnimInst from playing.  The given pointer should be considered invalid after this.

Params.Name: bImmediate
Params.Description: True to stop it right now and ignore blend out, false to let it blend out as indicated.

Functions.Name: StopAllCameraAnims
Functions.Description: Stop playing all CameraAnims on this CameraManager.

Params.Name: bImmediate
Params.Description: True to stop it right now and ignore blend out, false to let it blend out as indicated.
